title: Research
text: My research sits at the intersection of applied econometrics, health economics, and development economics, with most of my empirical work grounded in the South Asian — and particularly the Nepali — context.

title: Research
text:
Research positions

Research Consultant, Nepal Health Economics Association (NHEA) — 2026 – present
Research Associate, HERD International — 2025 – 2026
Research Assistant, Department of Economics, Kathmandu University — 2024 – 2025 (Supervisor: Dr. Santosh Adhikari)
Policy Research Fellow & Researcher, Samriddhi Foundation — 2024
Freelance Research Assistant, independent / remote — 2023 – present

title: Research
text:
Work in progress

Allocative and technical efficiency of Nepal’s health sector
Nepal Health Economics Association (NHEA) — ongoing
Benchmarks efficiency across providers and units in Nepal’s health system, drawing on provider-level and national survey data. The goal is evidence that can feed into health-financing and resource-allocation decisions in Nepal.


Gender norms, fertility, and female labour force participation
With Dr. Santosh Adhikari — ongoing
An independent research project on how social norms around gender and fertility shape women’s participation in the labour force in Nepal. Work on this began during my time as a Research Assistant at Kathmandu University and continues in collaboration with Dr. Santosh Adhikari.

title: Research
text:
Work-related research

Econometric modelling of sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) taxation
HERD International — 2025 – 2026
Contributed to modelling the economic and health effects of taxing sugar-sweetened beverages in Nepal. The work combined household expenditure data with price and consumption data to study demand responses, tax pass-through, and distributional consequences across different tax designs.


State Marginalization and the Onset of Maoist Insurgency in Nepal
Samriddhi Foundation — 2024. Supervisor: Ms. Arpita Nepal
A research manuscript on the political-economy roots of Nepal’s Maoist insurgency, with a focus on how patterns of state marginalization shaped conflict onset.


Local Governance Index for Madhesh Province
Samriddhi Foundation — 2024
Designed and built a Local Governance Index for Madhesh Province to benchmark governance performance across local units, alongside broader work on state marginalization and inclusion.

title: Research
text:
Undergraduate research works

The Caste-Based Wage Gap in Nepal: Extent, Trends, and Explanations
Undergraduate thesis, Kathmandu University — 2024 (work in progress). Supervisor: Dr. Mani Nepal
Uses nationally representative Nepali labour-market data to document the size and evolution of wage gaps between caste groups and decompose how much of the gap is accounted for by observable characteristics versus unexplained components.


Impact of Reservation Policy on the Education of Caste-Disadvantaged People in Nepal
Applied Economic Research course, Kathmandu University — 2023. Supervisor: Dr. Santosh Adhikari
Examines how reservation-based affirmative action policies have shaped educational attainment among historically disadvantaged caste groups in Nepal.


Balancing Growth and Sustainability: Carbon Emissions, Energy Consumption, and Economic Growth in South Asia
Presented at the 19th South Asian Economics Students’ Meet — 2024
A cross-country empirical study of the relationship between carbon emissions, energy consumption, and economic growth across South Asia.


Decoding Election 2022: Unveiling the Layer of Electoral Success
Empirical brief, with Subin Aryal — 2023
A short empirical brief analysing correlates of electoral success in Nepal’s 2022 general elections.

title: Research
text:
Conference presentations

19th South Asian Economics Students’ Meet, 2024
Tribhuvan University CAICE International Conference, 2023 and 2024
Harmony & Hues, interdepartmental conference, Kathmandu University

title: Arpan Acharya
text:
I am a researcher based in Kathmandu, Nepal. I completed my Bachelor’s in Economics from Kathmandu University in 2024, where I also worked as a Research Assistant at the Department of Economics after graduation. I am currently a consultant at the Nepal Health Economics Association (NHEA), working on applied economic research in the health sector.
As an aspiring PhD student, my research interests lie in causal inference, development economics, political economy, and health economics. My current and past work has touched on health-sector efficiency, sugar-sweetened beverage taxation, caste-based wage gaps, gender norms and female labour force participation, and the political economy of conflict in Nepal.
Eventually, I hope to pursue a PhD in Economics and return to work in academia in Nepal.
→ Read more about me, browse my research, or have a look at my CV.

title: About
text:
I’m currently a consultant at the Nepal Health Economics Association (NHEA), where I work with health-sector data and contribute to applied economic research — currently on the allocative and technical efficiency of Nepal’s health sector.
Alongside this, I’m pursuing a couple of independent research projects: one on gender norms, fertility, and female labour force participation in Nepal, and some exploratory work on the impact of conflict and social mobility in the Nepali context.
I also love teaching. I spend part of my time teaching econometrics and causal inference, and I take on occasional freelance research-support work for graduate students and professionals.

title: About
text: I’m an economist based in Kathmandu, Nepal. I completed a Bachelor’s degree in Economics at Kathmandu University (2020–2024), where I took coursework spanning micro and macroeconomics, econometrics, linear algebra, applied economic research, political economy, development economics, and international trade. During my undergrad I developed a strong interest in applied empirical work and in the institutional and historical roots of economic outcomes in South Asia.

title: About
text:
I’m currently a consultant at the Nepal Health Economics Association (NHEA), where I work with health-sector data and contribute to applied economic research — currently on the allocative and technical efficiency of Nepal’s health sector.
Alongside this, I’m pursuing a couple of independent research projects: one on gender norms, fertility, and female labour force participation in Nepal, and some exploratory work on the impact of conflict and social mobility in the Nepali context.
I also love teaching. I spend part of my time teaching econometrics and causal inference, and I take on occasional freelance research-support work for graduate students and professionals.

title: About
text:
Research interests
My primary fields are:

Causal inference and applied econometrics
Development economics
Political economy of conflict, institutions, and inclusion
Health economics

title: About
text:
Beyond economics
I’m broadly curious about how institutions, identity, and history shape economic life and I read across anthropology, political science, and history to inform my empirical work. I’m drawn to questions where the most interesting variation comes from social dynamics, political representation and systems of governance, and how these factors shape individual and collective responses to policy.

title: About
text:
Data and tools
I work mostly in R, Python, and Stata. I’ve also started folding AI coding agents into my day-to-day workflow. A lot of my research involves cleaning and scraping data sometimes as part of a job, sometimes for independent projects. And some of that code is up on my GitHub.

title: About
text:
Get in touch
The easiest way to reach me is by email. You can also find me on GitHub and LinkedIn.

title: Welcome To My Blog
text: This is the first post in a Quarto blog. Welcome!

title: Research
text:
Current work

Allocative and technical efficiency of Nepal’s health sector
Nepal Health Economics Association (NHEA) — 2026, ongoing
As a consultant with NHEA, I’m working on a project that measures allocative and technical efficiency across providers and units in Nepal’s health system. The work uses provider-level administrative data alongside national survey data to benchmark efficiency and examine what drives inefficiencies across geographies and facility types. The goal is to generate evidence that can feed into health-financing and resource-allocation decisions in Nepal.


Econometric modelling of sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) taxation
HERD International — 2025–2026
At HERD International I’ve contributed to modelling the economic and health effects of taxing sugar-sweetened beverages in Nepal. The work combines household-level expenditure data with price and consumption data to study demand responses, tax pass-through, and the distributional consequences of different tax designs. More broadly, I’ve been involved in econometric analysis of large-scale health survey datasets across a range of projects at HERD.

Impact of Reservation Policy on the Education of Caste-Disadvantaged People in Nepal
Applied Economic Research course, Kathmandu University — 2023 Supervisor: Dr. Santosh Adhikari
A research manuscript examining how reservation-based affirmative action policies have shaped educational attainment among historically disadvantaged caste groups in Nepal.


State Marginalization and the Onset of Maoist Insurgency in Nepal
Samriddhi Foundation — 2024 Supervisor: Ms. Arpita Nepal
A research manuscript on the political-economy roots of Nepal’s Maoist insurgency, with a focus on how patterns of state marginalization shaped conflict onset.


Balancing Growth and Sustainability: Carbon Emissions, Energy Consumption, and Economic Growth in South Asia
Presented at the 19th South Asian Economics Students’ Meet — 2024
A cross-country empirical examination of the relationship between carbon emissions, energy consumption, and economic growth in the South Asian context.

A short empirical brief analyzing correlates of electoral success in Nepal’s 2022 general elections.

title: Research
text:
Prior research positions

Research Assistant
Department of Economics, Kathmandu University — Dec 2024 – Dec 2025 Supervisor: Dr. Santosh Adhikari
Cleaned secondary survey datasets (NLSS, HRVS, NLFS, NDHS), reviewed literature for ongoing projects, supported the preparation of research outputs, and provided data and methods support to students.


Policy Research Fellow and Researcher
Samriddhi Foundation — May 2024 – Dec 2024
Built the Local Governance Index for Madhesh Province, conducted research on state marginalization and governance, and drafted manuscripts on conflict, inclusion, and governance.


Freelance Research Assistant
Independent, remote — 2023 – present
Providing research support — data cleaning, econometric analysis in Stata and R, and literature reviews — to graduate students at the University of Chicago, NYU Abu Dhabi, and Duke University.

title: Research
text:
Tools & data
Stata, R, Python, LaTeX, QGIS. Comfortable with Nepali survey datasets including NLSS, HRVS, NLFS, and NDHS, and with standard micro-econometric methods (OLS, IV, DiD, decomposition methods, limited dependent-variable models).
